Example: อดิศร one who is great อดิเรกgreater than one
อนุชนthose who follow, who come after อนุจรfollower
อัปยศ shame/disgrace อัปลักษณ์ ugly, low, devilish
อภิญญา with great knowledge อภิปรายto present in specific detail

Meaning: grand, over, large, on top of (Sometimes similar to ‘epi-‘ prefix in English)
Example: อธิปัญญา one with great intellect อธิษฐาน with great intent and focus (pray)

Meaning: descend, diminish, decrease (sometimes similar to ‘de-‘prefix in English)
Example: อวกาศwith decreased air/atmosphere (space) อวชาตบุตร a son who is inferior to his father

Meaning: entire, major, grand, opposite, enter, approach, near, secure
Example: อาคม approaching/come towards อาเทส changing
อุบัติto arise/happen อุโฆษ promote/give name to
อุปราช advisor to king อุปจาร almost/close to
ทรชน bad person ทรพร no energy
นิปริยายdirectly

Meaning: entire, in front of, before, sometimes similar to ‘pre- ‘prefix in English
Example: ประวัติ that which has happened ปัญญา overall knowledge / wisdom
ปฏิบัติapply/put into practice ปฏิทิน‘particular’ day – calendar

Meaning: cyclic, a cycle, region, all over
Example: ปริวัตรtranspose บริสุทธิ์the ‘n’th’ point/utmost = virgin/holy/innocent
วิจารณ์contemplate/research วิวรรณ beautiful complexion

Meaning: opposite (often similar to ‘contra-‘ or ‘anti-‘ prefixes in English)
Example: ปราชัยlose

Meaning: Without, outside of, sometimes similar to ‘ex-‘prefix in English.
Example: นิรนามhave no name นิรมลwithout blemish/beautiful

Meaning: along with, with, good (sometimes similar to ‘con-‘, ‘corr-‘ and ‘bene-‘ prefixes in English)
Example: สมาคมto come together สังคม to go together – society สนธิto connect

Meaning: good, beautiful, easy, sometimes similar to ‘bene-‘ or ‘facil-‘ prefixes in English
Example: สุชาดาof good birth สุเทพa good god/heavenly being สุพจน์ a good word
(rare – often found in Pali texts)
